commit_scope: Extend substrate surprise with missed-edge embodied memory and assemblage-shift guidance

local_validation.summary: Substrate surprise now asks what edge was missed and reports an embodied memory read for each surprise: missed-edge category, compressed edge reputation count, hot/cold temperature, whether to remember, felt body center, friction, benefit, circulation impact, memory storage mode, available recipes, and assemblage-point shift.

known_failed_attempts.output: zsh:1: no such file or directory: .venv/bin/pytest. The same focused test was run successfully with python3 -m pytest.

ci_validation.reason: No commit or PR created in this turn.

deploy_validation.reason: Local reporting and tests only; no runtime deployment was performed.

phase_gate.reason: Local validation passed, but no CI or deployment validation ran in this turn.

evidence_refs: make wellness passed and continues to call the shared substrate surprise formatter.
New tests assert semantic-adjacency surprises are hot remembered missed edges and domain-default surprises are remembered as category counts rather than pair memory.
